review_comment: Shouldn't `WEBSOCKET_FLAG_CLOSE` be handled separately? Otherwise, MQTT engine will not be aware that peer closed the connection. Socket based transport implementations simply return `0` in that case.

Sure, that can be added here. I just wonder that if we receive CLOSE, then the underlying socket is probably closed anyway by the peer so the end result is probably the same. Anyway, I will send new version.

[RFC 6455, chapter 7.1.2](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6455#section-7.1.2):

> Once an endpoint has both sent and received a Close control frame, that endpoint SHOULD _Close the WebSocket Connection_ as defined in Section 7.1.1.
